Tests the conversion of a non-zero number to a Boolean true value in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the `Boolean` function in JavaScript. It verifies that the number `11111` is correctly converted to the boolean value `true`. The test uses the `assert` function to ensure that the expression `true === Boolean(11111)` evaluates to `true`, confirming that non-zero numbers are truthy in JavaScript. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of type conversion in JavaScript.
